title: Malpighia hispaniolica
taxonomy: Malpighia hispaniolica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. This plant is native to the Dominican Republic. Malpighia hispaniolica is a type of tree or shrub that belongs to the genus Malpighia. The Malpighia hispaniolica is a member of the order Malpighiales. It is commonly known as a small tree with oval leaves and red berries.

intro: Malpighia hispaniolica, also known as the West Indian cherry or Jamaican cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties.
